ddls:
CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_code" TEXT /* Product code (e.g., IB2B12) */,
  "width" TEXT /* Width of the product (e.g., 1/2") */,
  "length" TEXT /* Length of the product (e.g., 27.5 YD) */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the product (e.g., Black, White) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category the product belongs to */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit of measure for the product */,
  "weight" TEXT /* Weight of the product */,
  "min_order_quantity" INTEGER /* Minimum order quantity for the product */,
  "storage_requirements" TEXT /* Storage requirements for the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Bundling Tape) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Parent category ID (for hierarchical categories) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory record */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse where the product is stored */,
  "current_stock" INTEGER /* Current stock level of the product */,
  "reorder_level" INTEGER /* Reorder level for the product */,
  "last_stock_check" TEXT /* Date of the last stock check */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the product within the warehouse */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the inventory (e.g., available, reserved, damaged) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "warehouses" (
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each warehouse */,
  "warehouse_name" TEXT /* Name of the warehouse */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the warehouse */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the warehouse */,
  "capacity" TEXT /* Capacity of the warehouse */,
  "dimensions" TEXT /* Dimensions of the warehouse */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the warehouse */,
  "manager" TEXT /* Manager or contact person for the warehouse */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production" (
  "production_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production record */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "batch_number" TEXT /* Batch number for the production run */,
  "production_date" TEXT /* Date of production */,
  "quantity_produced" INTEGER /* Quantity produced in the batch */,
  "quality_check_passed" INTEGER /* Whether the batch passed quality control checks */,
  "process" TEXT /* Production process or recipe */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Production notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("production_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer placing the order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., pending, shipped, canceled) */,
  "total_amount" TEXT /* Total amount of the order */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the order (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "status_history" TEXT /* Status history of the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_details" (
  "order_detail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order detail record */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sales order */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product ordered */,
  "unit_price" TEXT /* Unit price of the product */,
  "quantity_shipped" INTEGER /* Quantity shipped or delivered */,
  "shipping_date" TEXT /* Shipping or delivery date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_detail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_details_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_details_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES sales_orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer's company */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "credit_limit" TEXT /* Credit limit of the customer */,
  "payment_terms" TEXT /* Payment terms for the customer */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Shipping address of the customer */,
  "sales_representative" TEXT /* Sales representative or account manager for the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., manager, sales representative, production staff) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title or position of the user */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level or permissions of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_activity_log" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the activity */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the activity */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., login, logout, update, delete) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_activity_log_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
